title: Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia
tagline: Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Rama Lakshamana Temples or Samba Lakshamana Temples is the late 12th century twin Hindu temples in Baradia, a village in Okhamandal region of Devbhoomi Dwarka district, Gujarat
overview.description: The Rama Lakshamana Temples or Samba Lakshamana Temples is the late 12th century twin Hindu temples in Baradia, a village in Okhamandal region of Devbhoomi Dwarka district, Gujarat, India. Baradia is about five km southeast of Dwarka. There are some temples near the coast within the limits of the village.

topPlaces.name: Sudama Setu
topPlaces.description: Sudama Setu is a pedestrian suspension bridge in Dwarka, Gujarat, India. It is named for Sudama, a childhood friend of Krishna.

topPlaces.name: Dwarkadhish Temple
topPlaces.description: The Dwarkadhish temple, also known as the Jagat Mandir and occasionally spelled Dwarakadheesh, is a Hindu temple dedicated to Krishna, who is worshiped in the temple by the name Dwarkadhish (Sanskrit: द्वारकाधीश, IAST: Dvārakādhīśa, meaning: 'Lord of Dvārakā'). The temple is located in Dwarka city of Gujarat, India, which is one of the destinations of Char Dham, a Hindu pilgrimage circuit.

topPlaces.name: Dwarka
topPlaces.description: Dwarka () is a coastal town and municipality of Devbhumi Dwarka district in the Indian state of Gujarat. It is located on the western shore of the Okhamandal Peninsula on the right bank of the Gomti River at the mouth of the Gulf of Kutch facing the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Kalika Mata Temple, New Dhrewad
topPlaces.description: The Kalika Mata Temple is located at New Dhrewad in Devbhoomi Dwarka district, Gujarat, India.

items.note: Sudama Setu is a pedestrian suspension bridge in Dwarka, Gujarat, India. It is named for Sudama, a childhood friend of Krishna.

The Dwarkadhish temple, also known as the Jagat Mandir and occasionally spelled Dwarakadheesh, is a Hindu temple dedicated to Krishna, who is worshiped in the temple by the name Dwarkadhish (Sanskrit: द्वारकाधीश, IAST: Dvārakādhīśa, meaning: 'Lord of Dvārakā'). The temple is located in Dwarka city of Gujarat, India, which is one of the destinations of Char Dham, a Hindu pilgrimage circuit.

Dwarka () is a coastal town and municipality of Devbhumi Dwarka district in the Indian state of Gujarat. It is located on the western shore of the Okhamandal Peninsula on the right bank of the Gomti River at the mouth of the Gulf of Kutch facing the Arabian Sea.
The Kalika Mata Temple is located at New Dhrewad in Devbhoomi Dwarka district, Gujarat, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Porbandar Airport (91 km) and the nearest railway station is Rajkot Junction (183 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia?
faq.a: Stays in Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rama Lakshamana Temples, Baradia?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sudama Setu, Dwarkadhish Temple, Dwarka, Kalika Mata Temple, New Dhrewad.
